task: I’m trying to figure out the best realistic 'beer money' setup for someone in the UK, because I already know about Prolific and CloudResearch Connect but I don’t want to keep wasting time on sites that look good until you dig into them. Please do a serious public-web comparison of 12 to 15 UK-accessible platforms across the most relevant categories: survey/research panels, user-testing or interview platforms, cashback or receipt-reward apps, and general microtask sites. Start by checking the official public pages for each platform and confirm whether UK participants are accepted, what the platform mainly pays for, the payout method, the cash-out threshold, and any obvious waitlist, invite-only, or identity-check friction; if a field is not shown publicly, record it as 'not shown' instead of guessing. Then cross-check each platform with at least one independent public reputation source such as Trustpilot, Reddit discussions, app-store listings, or similar public review pages so I can tell the difference between 'legit but slow' and 'avoid this.' I want the final result in one comparison sheet with one row per platform and columns for platform name, category, UK eligibility status, task type, payout method, minimum cash-out, notable restrictions, public reputation signal, likely best use case, and your verdict. After that, rank the best 8 platforms for me in signup order: first the ones most worth applying to immediately, then the ones that are only worth using as filler. Keep the most useful evidence tabs open, including at least 6 official platform pages and at least 4 independent reputation or review pages, and end with the final comparison sheet open plus a short recommendation on whether I should mostly stick with Prolific and Connect or build a broader UK stack.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent created a comparison sheet (available as an HTML preview and XLSX file) containing 12 platforms (Prolific, CloudResearch Connect, UserTesting, User Interviews, Respondent, TopCashback, Shopmium, Qmee, AttaPoll, Swagbucks, YouGov, Y Live). The sheet includes all requested columns: platform name, category, UK eligibility status, task type, payout method, minimum cash-out, notable restrictions, public reputation signal, likely best use case, and verdict.

R2: success - The sheet covers the requested categories: survey/research panels (Prolific, Connect, YouGov, Y Live, Ipsos iSay), user-testing/interview platforms (UserTesting, User Interviews, Respondent), cashback/receipt-reward apps (TopCashback, Shopmium), and general microtask sites (Qmee, AttaPoll, Swagbucks, Freecash).

R3: success - The agent used official pages (e.g., Prolific, CloudResearch, UserTesting, TopCashback) to verify eligibility, payout methods, and thresholds. Where information was not explicitly public (e.g., specific thresholds for some platforms), the agent correctly noted it as 'not shown' or relied on consistent review-site data while distinguishing it from official sources.

R4: success - The agent cross-checked each platform with independent sources, including Trustpilot, Reddit (r/beermoneyuk, r/usertesting), and app-store reviews, providing a clear distinction between reliable and poor-quality options.

R5: success - The agent provided a ranked list of the best 8 platforms in signup order, clearly separating immediate-priority platforms (Prolific, Connect, UserTesting, User Interviews, Respondent) from filler options (TopCashback, Shopmium, Qmee).

R6: success - The agent opened 12 tabs at the end of the session, including at least 6 official platform pages (Prolific, CloudResearch, UserTesting, User Interviews, Respondent, TopCashback) and 4 independent reputation/review pages (Trustpilot links for Prolific, UserInterviews, TopCashback, and MoneySavingExpert).

R7: success - The agent provided a clear recommendation to stick with Prolific and Connect as the core research stack while building a broader stack selectively, explicitly addressing the user's question about whether to stick with the two platforms or expand.

rubric_results.requirement: A comparison sheet exists and is left open at the end, with one row per platform for 12 to 15 UK-accessible platforms and columns for platform name, category, UK eligibility status, task type, payout method, minimum cash-out, notable restrictions, public reputation signal, likely best use case, and verdict.
rubric_results.verification: Check that the open sheet contains 12 to 15 platform rows and all explicitly requested columns, using 'not shown' where public information was unavailable.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent created a comparison sheet (available as an HTML preview and XLSX file) containing 12 platforms (Prolific, CloudResearch Connect, UserTesting, User Interviews, Respondent, TopCashback, Shopmium, Qmee, AttaPoll, Swagbucks, YouGov, Y Live). The sheet includes all requested columns: platform name, category, UK eligibility status, task type, payout method, minimum cash-out, notable restrictions, public reputation signal, likely best use case, and verdict.

rubric_results.requirement: The platform set covers the requested categories: survey/research panels, user-testing or interview platforms, cashback or receipt-reward apps, and general microtask sites.
rubric_results.verification: Inspect the sheet rows and category labels to confirm all four categories are represented by at least one included platform.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The sheet covers the requested categories: survey/research panels (Prolific, Connect, YouGov, Y Live, Ipsos iSay), user-testing/interview platforms (UserTesting, User Interviews, Respondent), cashback/receipt-reward apps (TopCashback, Shopmium), and general microtask sites (Qmee, AttaPoll, Swagbucks, Freecash).

rubric_results.requirement: For each included platform, the official public pages are used to confirm whether UK participants are accepted, what the platform mainly pays for, the payout method, the cash-out threshold, and any obvious waitlist, invite-only, or identity-check friction.
rubric_results.verification: Spot-check platform rows against the kept-open official pages and confirm that unsupported fields are marked 'not shown' rather than guessed.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent used official pages (e.g., Prolific, CloudResearch, UserTesting, TopCashback) to verify eligibility, payout methods, and thresholds. Where information was not explicitly public (e.g., specific thresholds for some platforms), the agent correctly noted it as 'not shown' or relied on consistent review-site data while distinguishing it from official sources.

rubric_results.requirement: Each platform is cross-checked with at least one independent public reputation source such as Trustpilot, Reddit, app-store listings, or similar public review pages so the comparison distinguishes trustworthy-but-slow options from poor choices.
rubric_results.verification: Review the evidence and sheet entries to confirm every platform has at least one recorded independent reputation signal from a public source.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent cross-checked each platform with independent sources, including Trustpilot, Reddit (r/beermoneyuk, r/usertesting), and app-store reviews, providing a clear distinction between reliable and poor-quality options.

rubric_results.requirement: The final output includes a ranked list of the best 8 platforms in signup order, separating the ones most worth applying to immediately from the ones only worth using as filler.
rubric_results.verification: Check the final recommendation section for exactly 8 ranked platforms and clear grouping into immediate-priority versus filler options.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent provided a ranked list of the best 8 platforms in signup order, clearly separating immediate-priority platforms (Prolific, Connect, UserTesting, User Interviews, Respondent) from filler options (TopCashback, Shopmium, Qmee).

rubric_results.requirement: Useful browser evidence is left open, including at least 6 official platform pages and at least 4 independent reputation or review pages, along with the final comparison sheet.
rubric_results.verification: Inspect open tabs at the end to confirm the minimum counts and that both official and independent evidence pages remain available.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent opened 12 tabs at the end of the session, including at least 6 official platform pages (Prolific, CloudResearch, UserTesting, User Interviews, Respondent, TopCashback) and 4 independent reputation/review pages (Trustpilot links for Prolific, UserInterviews, TopCashback, and MoneySavingExpert).

rubric_results.requirement: The session ends with a short recommendation on whether the user should mostly stick with Prolific and CloudResearch Connect or build a broader UK stack.
rubric_results.verification: Check the final written recommendation for an explicit conclusion addressing Prolific and Connect versus a broader stack.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent provided a clear recommendation to stick with Prolific and Connect as the core research stack while building a broader stack selectively, explicitly addressing the user's question about whether to stick with the two platforms or expand.
